content.problemStatement:
Chef has a machine which he uses to rotate sequences. If he puts a sequence S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​ into the machine, it produces the sequence S2,S3,…,SN,S1S_2, S_3, \ldots, S_N, S_1S2​,S3​,…,SN​,S1​, i.e. the first element of the sequence is moved to the end.

Chef is definitely not a newbie ― he knows about trivial things like finding the maximum sum of a contiguous subsequence. Therefore, he now made a difficult problem to challenge himself:

You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each kkk (0≤k≤N−10 \le k \le N-10≤k≤N−1), consider the sequence produced by inserting it into the machine repeatedly kkk times (i.e. inserting AAA into the machine and replacing AAA by the sequence it produces, kkk times); find the maximum sum of a non empty contiguous subsequence of this sequence.

However, to solve this problem, Chef needs the help of a pro. Solve it for him.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should denote the largest sum of a non empty contiguous subsequence of the sequence produced by the machine after i−1i-1i−1 repeated insertions.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

After zero insertions, A=[−5,4,1,2]A = [-5, 4, 1, 2]A=[−5,4,1,2]. The contiguous subsequence with the maximum sum is A=[4,1,2]A = [4, 1, 2]A=[4,1,2].
After one insertion, A=[4,1,2,−5]A = [4, 1, 2, -5]A=[4,1,2,−5]. The contiguous subsequence with the maximum sum is A=[4,1,2]A = [4, 1, 2]A=[4,1,2].
After two insertions, A=[1,2,−5,4]A = [1, 2, -5, 4]A=[1,2,−5,4]. The contiguous subsequence with the maximum sum is A=[4]A = [4]A=[4].
After three insertions, A=[2,−5,4,1]A = [2, -5, 4, 1]A=[2,−5,4,1]. The contiguous subsequence with the maximum sum is A=[4,1]A = [4, 1]A=[4,1].
